generated_oml:
// C1: Physical System Component
instance RobotMabiMax100 : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Serial industrial robot MABI MAX 100 with six axes, each driven by a PMSM and a cycloidal gear transmission, used for high‑precision machining in a cyber‑physical production environment."
]

// C3: Physical sensing components – position encoders (primary & secondary) for each axis
instance PrimaryEncoderAxis1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary position encoder mounted on the PMSM shaft of Axis 1, providing high‑resolution motor‑side angle measurements."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionDataTxPrimaryAxis1
]
instance SecondaryEncoderAxis1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder located after the gearbox of Axis 1, delivering joint‑side position data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionDataTxSecondaryAxis1
]

// Additional sensing components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [

]

    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentDataTx
]

]
instance CurrentDataTx : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

// C5: Data objects (time‑series traces) stored in the digital shadow
instance PositionDataPrimaryAxis1 : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑series of motor‑side angle for Axis 1."

// C10: Models used by the processing component
instance SimulinkFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics‑based Simulink model of robot joint friction using analytical friction equations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessingComponent
]
instance NeuralNetworkFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data‑driven neural‑network model trained on streamed sensor data for friction estimation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessingComponent
]

// C11: Enabler – processing component that consumes data and runs models
instance ProcessingComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing component consumes input data traces, applies Simulink and neural‑network models, and emits higher‑level output traces with provenance (originates‑from)."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeFrictionCompensationService,
                         PredictiveMaintenanceService,
                         MotionAccuracyOptimizationService
]

// C6: Services offered by the digital twin
instance RealTimeFrictionCompensationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides continuous multi‑physics‑aware identification of joint friction for real‑time compensation."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCompensationInsight
]
instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predicts future joint wear and issues maintenance recommendations."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]
instance MotionAccuracyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimizes robot motion accuracy by updating model parameters and retraining neural networks."
    DTDFVocab:provides AccuracyImprovementInsight,
                         AccuracyImprovementAction
]

// C17: Insights and actions derived from services
instance FrictionCompensationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into current friction levels enabling immediate compensation during motion."
]
instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action advising scheduled maintenance based on predicted wear."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance AccuracyImprovementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight on potential accuracy degradation and required parameter updates."
]
instance AccuracyImprovementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action to recalibrate controller set‑points for improved positioning accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C8: Enablers for communication infrastructure (optional, not part of core flow)
instance MQTTBrokerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT publish/subscribe broker (e.g., Mosquitto) delivering one‑way sensor data streams to the digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables ProcessingComponent
]
instance ThingsBoardPersistenceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ThingsBoard instance persisting output traces generated by the processing component."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeFrictionCompensationService,
                         PredictiveMaintenanceService,
                         MotionAccuracyOptimizationService
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system is orchestrated according to the Digital Shadow Reference Model. It comprises a data‑acquisition layer that collects raw data traces, a processing component that consumes these traces using engineering, simulation or data‑calculation models, and a storage layer that holds the resulting computed traces. Relationships such as the ‘originates‑from’ association maintain traceability across components. The concrete digital shadow model is a conforming realization of the reference model, mapping its abstract concepts to concrete classes and associations, thus defining the integration of acquisition, processing, and representation components within a unified architecture."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin is developed following Model‑Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) where the system description is created in successive abstraction levels: requirements model, functional architecture model, logical architecture model, and product/technical layer model. These stages correspond to development phases and are managed by mapping each phase to a concrete representation (e.g., SysML requirement diagrams, SysML internal block diagrams for functional and logical architecture, and detailed engineering models for the product layer). Transition between phases is handled by traceability links defined in the reference model and by the ‘originates‑from’ association that connects data traces to the processing components that generated them. Lifecycle governance is implied by the use of reference models with a defined conformance relation that identifies which concrete models belong to the DT at each stage."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is required to meet the real‑time constraints of the robot control loop; therefore the computational latency (dead time) of the simulation and neural‑network inference must be bounded. Validation is performed by traceability of each data trace to its originating processing component and input sources, as defined by the ‘originates‑from’ association. Verification of model transformations (e.g., SysML to Simulink) is emphasized to ensure correctness of the white‑box models. Uncertainty sources such as sensor noise, temperature variation and wear are mitigated by continuous model adaptation: friction model parameters are updated online and neural networks are retrained with the latest data. Quality assurance includes systematic verification of transformations and traceability checks, as highlighted in the digital‑shadow reference model."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Communication between the physical sensors and the digital shadow relies on the MQTT protocol (client‑broker model) as referenced by the Mosquitto server/client implementation. MQTT operates over TCP/IP, providing a publish/subscribe mechanism for the time‑series sensor streams. The documents do not specify the network topology, bandwidth, latency, redundancy, or security mechanisms beyond the use of MQTT."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT uses established modelling standards: SysML (requirements, block diagrams, internal block diagrams), AADL (architecture analysis), ACME (component‑connector ADL), and MontiArc‑Automaton for behaviour specifications. Conformance to the Digital Shadow Reference Model is defined by a precise conformance relation that maps concrete classes and associations to the reference class diagram, thereby enforcing interoperability between models. The approach also aligns with the MBSE practices and with the standards adopted in the cited works on Model‑Driven Engineering for Digital Twins."
]
